item.title: Re: +1, but with a few format errors
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much for the kind words and the upvote!

I have now addressed the issues you had pointed out, my thanks for your feedback it's much appreciated. I'm still a little unsure on that last part you mentioned, I've fumbled with the wording and I think it's more clear now, but I'll come back to it later and ensure that is the case when I'm not under time constraints.

Thank you again!

item.title: +1, but with a few format errors
item.content:encoded:
The images, voice recordings, and format in general were very well created for this scip. I also love the idea of the scip itself; an influence that controls its victims to the point where even the researchers assigned to the scip question their sanity.

Just a few minor formatting errors and typoes:

"The dexterity of the SCP-4965-1's movement"
Describing the anomalies as "instances of SCP-4965-1" rather than "SCP-4965-1s" fits more into SCP document syntax.

In one of the logs, the question "How have you been effecting the monitoring equipment and security systems" is asked.
The "effecting" should be replaced with "affecting".

In the special containment procedures, you write "All residents are screened for SCP-4965 and all infected humans become designated as SCP-4965-1".
This sentence feels moderately repetitive. Maybe seperate it into two sentences and describe the effect the SCP has on its hosts? And remember, the hosts are instances of SCP-4965-1 before they are identified. They aren't instances only after being found, which the sentence confusingly suggests.
If you want to keep this part in one sentence, I'd suggest using something like "All residents are screened and examined for characteristics of SCP-4965-1. SCP-4965-1 is the term used for instances…" and so on and so forth.

Overall, good job! I look forward to seeing more scips written by you in the near future.
Liked this as a draft, like it here. +1

item.title: Re: Good idea
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the kind words and the upvote! I very much appreciate you taking the time to read and reply.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My apologies, the intent was more to present the various different formats as providing the sense that the documents had been scanned in; hence the more formal research proposals and the handwritten note; but I can absolutely see how it may be jarring and I'm sorry for that!

And apologies for the misplaced attributions also, this is my first article and I seem to have missed that detail in the various guides, entirely my own fault and it has now been amended. My bad!

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Ah, my apologies I was unaware of that nuance! I shall replace with a compliant image ASAP.

item.title: Acknowledgements
item.content:encoded:
Show Acknowledgements
Hide Acknowledgements

Image1: [https://live.staticflickr.com/7615/16930013551_7326de1492_b.jpg] "Watch your step" by Carlos Ebert is licensed under CC BY 2.0 and edited by Daniel Moss (me)
Image2: [https://farm2.staticflickr.com/1080/537103907_cff96d9cbb_b.jpg] is licensed under CC BY 2.0
Image3: [https://live.staticflickr.com/8362/8292845428_cc0ec84eca_b.jpg] "Oskar Negt" by kaffeeringe is licensed under CC BY 2.0 and edited by Daniel Moss (me)

My deepest thanks are extended to:
AKFrost and Mew-1tiverse for greenlighting and feedback.

Wolfen32, SamWeston, MaliceAF, UndeadHippo, S150wned, Bjonk, LukeLukens, numbersAreImaginary for feedback.

My sincerest apologies but I am aware that I am missing the names of at least two people who provided feedback, stupidly I didn't think to take down names from IRC as this is my first skip. If you know you did and would like credit, please do message me and I shall amend this post!

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The third image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) as the license of the original image contains “NC” (non-commercial), making it non-compliant with the commercial SCP wiki. Thus, the image has been removed.

EDIT: The image has been replaced with a cc-compliant alternative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My problem with this is that it has so many different formats introduced that it looks like an hors d'oeuvres platter of alternative formats and logs; it is jarring to read it and have it look like a showcase of different kinds of formatting taken from the Style Resource page. Pick one (or two, at the very least) and stick with it. The inconsistency is very apparent and it just makes the entire article a messy mish-mash of formats.

It's also very distracting to put your attributions in the article. It doesn't make any sense and it ruins whatever investment any reader would've had from reading the article up until that point.

item.title: Good idea
item.content:encoded:
The idea behind this SCP is very good since it is a twist on the common mind control virus. The formatting is also good and doesn't make the post seem too long.

This earned an upvote from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: FYI: Image and critter acknowledgments go in the discussion page.
